name: blueprint
voice: drafting-table cyanotype — captions are a technical drawing. The lower third is a faint drafting band with a bright rule and a title block; transcript lines sit LEFT-ALIGNED on thin baseline rules that draw L→R, words WIPE in along the line with a registration tick at each word, and emphasis words get a dimension callout (bracket + tiny mono label). At the apex a cyanotype scrim + drafting grid charge behind the subject and a thin white PLOTTER pen writes the hero word stroke by stroke (square nib, construction guide, registration crosses), then a 45° hatch-fill sweeps through the glyph and a 'REV A // APPROVED' chip stamps the corner.
when: engineering, science, data, architecture, product-spec and explainer content — anything precise and measured that wants a measured-not-loud register. The cool cyanotype scrim supplies its own contrast on bright scenes; reads as a working drawing, so it suits deliberate pacing.
